conditions.critical_condition_basis: critical condition of the corresponding homopolymer
conditions.evidence_text: Chang and coworkers [21] investigated the separation of deuterated PS-graft-hydrogenous PS (hPS-g-dPS) copolymers having a single hPS backbone and a few dPS side chains. In that work, the graft copolymer is separated under conditions in which dPS branches are at the critical condition and are purportedly chromatographically invisible
conditions.notes: Experimental study cited in the introduction; specific mobile phase composition not provided in text.

conditions.critical_condition_basis: critical condition of the corresponding homopolymer
conditions.evidence_text: Chang and coworkers [22] examined the retention behavior of a hPS-b-dPS block copolymer under similar conditions and observed the same interesting retention behavior, namely, a decrease in retention time as the invisible block length increases when the overall retention of the block copolymer is in LAC mode.
conditions.notes: Experimental study cited in the introduction; specific mobile phase composition not provided in text.

conditions.evidence_text: The B side chains are made 'invisible' by setting w(B) to the critical condition of a homopolymer. Our previous simulation studies [23-25] have established that the polymer surface interaction energy at the critical condition, wcc, is -0.18232 for an RW chain and -0.276 for a SAW chain, respectively.
conditions.notes: Monte Carlo simulation setup described in Section 2. Pore size is defined as a slit pore with dimensions 250a x 250a x 30a.
